baseline.reason: Accurately identifies the CWE-401 memory leak root cause: memory allocated by png_create_read_struct (line 582) leaks when main's return 99 (line 964) bypasses display_clean (line 990) and display_destroy (line 994). Correctly traces the longjmp-based control flow from display_log through do_test's setjmp return code to main's early exit. Minor omission: does not mention the do_test struct member change visible in the fix.

vastdb.reason: Contains a substantive inaccuracy: 'no such deallocation function is ever called in the codebase' is false — display_clean/display_destroy exist and are called on normal paths; the leak is specifically from the return-99 bypass. Also conflates line-947 unknown-option return (which occurs before any allocation) with the real leak path at line 964. Trigger path correctly identifies png_create_read_struct allocation and return 99 sink, but the incorrect claim about missing dealloc functions undermines the root cause analysis.
